title: Your first session
prompt: Your first session — a gentle welcome into the practice.
script:
Hey. So glad you're here. <break time="3s" />

Let's start by getting comfortable — sitting, lying down, whatever feels right for your body in this moment. <break time="3s" />

If your eyes want to close, let them. <break time="3s" />

Now, take one of your hands, and gently place a single finger right on your belly button. <break time="6s" />

That's our anchor for today. <break time="3s" />

The whole practice, really. Anytime your mind starts to wander off — and it will, that's just what minds do — you come back here, to this little spot under your finger. <break time="3s" />

Breathe in toward that finger. <break time="3s" />

And let the breath out slowly. <break time="3s" />

Notice how your belly rises into your fingertip on the inhale, and softens away on the exhale. <break time="6s" />

That's it. That tiny point of contact is the whole map. <break time="3s" />

If a thought shows up, or an itch, or a sound pulls you away — no problem. Just notice, and tune back into the belly button. <break time="3s" />

Good. Finger still resting on the belly button. <break time="3s" />

Now we're gonna build the breath in layers, slowly. <break time="3s" />

Breathe in gently, just down into the belly — feel it press out against your finger. <break time="6s" />

And out. <break time="3s" />

This next breath, take it a little deeper. Belly first, then let it rise into your lungs — feel the ribs widen. <break time="6s" />

And soften out. <break time="3s" />

One more layer. Breathe in — belly, lungs, and now imagine the breath filling the space behind your heart. <break time="3s" />

Our lungs go into our backs too, and we tend to forget about that whole area. <break time="6s" />

Let it fill there. <break time="3s" />

And release. <break time="3s" />

Now if you want, on this next inhale, send the breath all the way down to your toes — like a slow wave moving through you. <break time="6s" />

Notice the cool air at your nostrils on the way in. Warmer on the way out. <break time="6s" />

You can pause for a second at the top if you like — let the oxygen do its quiet work. <break time="6s" />

And keep that finger on the belly button. That's home base. <break time="3s" />

Now we let the breath go. <break time="6s" /> No more shaping it, no more guiding it — it just breathes itself. <break time="9s" /> Your finger's still resting on your belly button, and that's the home base. <break time="6s" /> If a thought floats in, let it. If your shoulder twitches, let it. Nothing here needs fixing. <break time="6s" /> I'm just sitting here breathing too, noticing whatever shows up. <break time="6s" /> And somewhere in this quiet, see if a small intention wants to surface — a word, a feeling, what you'd like this practice to be for you. <break time="9s" /> No need to chase it. Just leave the door open. <break time="6s" /> And keep coming back to that fingertip on the belly. <break time="6s" />

Let's start to gently come back. <break time="6s" />

Keep your finger right there on your belly button for a moment longer, feeling it rise and fall. <break time="6s" />

That little point of contact — that's your anchor. <break time="3s" />

It's been there this whole time, and it'll be there whenever you need it again. <break time="6s" />

Notice the sounds in the room around you. <break time="3s" />

The weight of your body wherever you're sitting or lying. <break time="6s" />

And take a moment to acknowledge that you actually did this — you sat down, you breathed, you showed up for yourself. <break time="6s" />

That's not a small thing, especially the first time. <break time="6s" />

Whenever life gets loud today, you can place a finger on your belly button, take one slow breath in… <break time="3s" /> and out. <break time="6s" />

That's the whole practice. It's always with you. <break time="6s" />

When you're ready, gently open your eyes.
